maml:description.maml:para: Gets the PSAction1 module debug mode state for the current PowerShell session.
`Get-Action1Debug` returns the debug mode state stored by `Set-Action1Debug`.
The value is stored only for the duration of the current PowerShell session.
You cannot pipe objects to this command.
The current PSAction1 module debug mode state.

maml:alert.maml:para: Debug mode affects only the current PowerShell session and is stored in a script-scope variable.

command:example.maml:title: ------------- Example 1: Get the debug mode state -------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns `$true` when debug mode is enabled and `$false` when debug mode is disabled.

command:example.maml:title: -------- Example 2: Set and verify the debug mode state --------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Action1Debug -Enabled $true PS C:\> Get-Action1Debug True

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables debug mode for PSAction1 module, then returns the configured state.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the default Action1 organization ID for the current PowerShell session.
`Get-Action1DefaultOrgId` returns the organization ID stored by `Set-Action1DefaultOrg`.
The value is stored only for the duration of the current PowerShell session.
The default organization ID, or no output when a default organization is not configured.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Get the default organization ID ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns the currently configured default organization ID.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the default Action1 organization name for the current PowerShell session.
`Get-Action1DefaultOrgName` returns the organization name stored by `Set-Action1DefaultOrg`.
The value is stored only for the duration of the current PowerShell session.
The default organization name, or no output when a default organization is not configured.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get the default organization name ---------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns the currently configured default organization name.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets Action1 organizations available to the current account.
`Get-Action1Organizations` calls the Action1 organizations endpoint and returns a sorted object list containing organization names and IDs.
Results are sorted by organization name, then organization ID.
Each object contains `Org_Name` and `Org_ID` properties.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns organization names and IDs available to the current account.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the configured Action1 API region for the current PowerShell session.
`Get-Action1Region` returns the Action1 API region configured by `Set-Action1Region`.
The value is stored only for the duration of the current PowerShell session.
If the region name is not available but the module has a configured API base URI, the command tries to resolve the region name from the module's internal host lookup table.
You cannot pipe objects to this command.
The configured Action1 API region, or no output when a region is not configured.

maml:alert.maml:para: `Get-Action1Region` returns the region name, not the full Action1 API base URI.
When the region is inferred from the configured API base URI, duplicate host aliases resolve to the first matching region in the module's internal host lookup table.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get the configured Action1 region ---------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns the currently configured Action1 API region.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Set and verify the Action1 region ---------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Action1Region -Region Europe PS C:\> Get-Action1Region Europe

dev:remarks.maml:para: Configures the PSAction1 module to use the Europe API endpoint, then returns the configured region.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets Action1 API credentials for the current PowerShell session.
Stores Action1 APIKey and Secret in script scope variables used by other commands in the module to authenticate API calls.
Action1 API key provided by the service.
Action1 API secret associated with the Action1 API key.
Action1 API key provided by the service.
Action1 API secret associated with the Action1 API key.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command only sets module and session variables.

maml:alert.maml:para: Action1 credentials are stored only for the lifetime of the PowerShell session.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Stores Action1 credentials for use by other PSAction1 module commands.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables or disables debug mode for PSAction1 module.
Sets the internal debug flag for the PSAction1 module in script scope.
When debug mode is enabled, additional diagnostic messages will be displayed by module functions that support debugging output.
Specifies whether the debug mode should be enabled or disabled.
Specifies whether the debug mode should be enabled or disabled.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command does not return objects. It only updates an internal module variable controlling debug behavior.

maml:alert.maml:para: The debug mode affects only the current PowerShell session and is stored in a script-scope variable.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables the debug mode for PSAction1 module.
Disables the debug mode for PSAction1 module.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the default Action1 organization for the current PowerShell session.
`Set-Action1DefaultOrg` configures the default Action1 organization context used by other commands in the module.
The command supports two ways to set the default organization:
* By organization ID using `-Org_ID`.
* By unique organization name using `-Org_Name`.
When `-Org_ID` is used, the command resolves the organization by ID and stores both the organization ID and organization name as the default organization context.

When `-Org_Name` is used, the command retrieves the available organizations, searches for an organization with the specified name, and stores both the matching organization's ID and name as the default organization context.
The default organization value is stored only for the duration of the current PowerShell session.
Specifies the Action1 organization ID to use as the default organization for subsequent module commands.
The command resolves the ID through the API and stores the matching organization ID and name.
Specifies the Action1 organization name to resolve and use as the default organization.
The command searches the available organizations for an exact name match. The name must match exactly and must identify a single organization.
If no organization is found, the command returns a terminating error.
If multiple organizations have the same name, the command returns a terminating error and instructs the user to specify the organization ID with `-Org_ID`.
Using this parameter requires valid Action1 authentication and access to list organizations.
Specifies the Action1 organization ID to use as the default organization for subsequent module commands.
The command resolves the ID through the API and stores the matching organization ID and name.
Specifies the Action1 organization name to resolve and use as the default organization.
The command searches the available organizations for an exact name match. The name must match exactly and must identify a single organization.
If no organization is found, the command returns a terminating error.
If multiple organizations have the same name, the command returns a terminating error and instructs the user to specify the organization ID with `-Org_ID`.
Using this parameter requires valid Action1 authentication and access to list organizations.
You cannot pipe objects to this command.
This command does not produce output. It sets a module-level default organization value for the current PowerShell session.

maml:alert.maml:para: The default organization value is stored in a script scope variable and is available only for the lifetime of the current PowerShell session.
`-Org_ID` is the most deterministic option and should be preferred in automation scripts.
`-Org_Name` is provided for convenience in interactive scenarios and scripts where organization names are known to be unique.

command:example.maml:title: -------- Example 1: Set the default organization by ID --------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Resolves the organization by ID and sets it as the default organization.
This is the recommended option for automation scripts because organization IDs are unique and unambiguous.

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 2: Set the default organization by name -------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds an organization named `MyOrgName` and stores its organization ID and name as the default organization context.
The organization name must be unique. If more than one organization has the specified name, the command returns a terminating error and the organization ID must be used instead.
Uses the `OrgId` alias for `-Org_ID`.
Uses the `OrgName` alias for `-Org_Name`.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables or disables interactive mode for PSAction1 module.
Enables or disables interactive mode for the current PowerShell session.

When interactive mode is enabled, the PSAction1 module will prompt for the required variable values that have not been set. This helps guide users through configuration when running commands that depend on module-level variables.
When interactive mode is disabled, commands will not prompt for missing values and may fail if required variables are not set.
Action1 interactive setting is stored in a script-scoped variable and applies only to the current PowerShell session.
Specifies whether PSAction1 interactive mode should be enabled or disabled.
Specifies whether PSAction1 interactive mode should be enabled or disabled.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command only modifies a module-level setting.

maml:alert.maml:para: The interactive mode is stored in a script-scoped variable and remains active only for the lifetime of the PowerShell session.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables interactive mode. The module will prompt for required variables that are missing.
Disables interactive mode so commands will not prompt for missing variables.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the Action1 API region endpoint used by the PSAction1 module.
`Set-Action1Region` configures the base Action1 API endpoint used by the PSAction1 module for subsequent requests in the current PowerShell session.
The command updates the module's internal region setting by selecting one of the supported regional API endpoints from the module's internal host lookup table.
Run this command before calling Action1 API commands when you need to target a specific Action1 regional environment.
Specifies the Action1 API region to use.
Accepted values are:
- NorthAmerica
- NorthAmerica-2
- NA-2
- Europe
- Australia
Specifies the Action1 API region to use.
Accepted values are:
- NorthAmerica
- NorthAmerica-2
- NA-2
- Europe
- Australia
You cannot pipe objects to this command.
This command does not return output. It sets an internal module variable used to determine the Action1 API base URI for the current PowerShell session.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command affects all subsequent PSAction1 commands in the current PowerShell session that use the module's configured Action1 API base URI.
Use this command before making API requests if the default regional endpoint is not the one you want to target.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Configures the PSAction1 module to use the Europe API endpoint.
Configures the PSAction1 module to use the North America API endpoint.
Configures the PSAction1 module to use the Australia API endpoint.
Configures the PSAction1 module to use the NorthAmerica-2 API endpoint.
Configures the PSAction1 module to use the NA-2 API endpoint value.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves Action1 objects, reports, raw API responses, and configuration templates.

The `Get-Action1` function retrieves Action1 objects and data from the Action1 API. It can also return helper template objects used to create or modify Action1 resources with commands such as `New-Action1` and `Update-Action1`.

Depending on the value of the `Query` parameter, the command can retrieve Action1 resources such as endpoints, endpoint groups, automations, policies, reports, packages, vulnerabilities, and the current user information.
When `Query` is set to `RawURI`, the command performs a direct GET request against the API path specified by the `URI` parameter.

When `Query` is set to `Settings`, the command returns a template object for the resource type specified by the `For` parameter. For supported resource types, the returned template can include helper script methods for modifying the object before sending it back to the API.
For long list queries, pagination is handled automatically. If the `Limit` parameter is not specified, the function uses a page size of 200.
Some returned objects include helper methods for convenience. Policy result objects include a `GetDetails()` method. Endpoint-related objects include a `GetCustomAttribute()` method.
The default Action1 organization should be configured before running organization-scoped queries. Use `Set-Action1DefaultOrg` to configure the default organization.
Specifies the type of Action1 object, data set, raw API request, or template to retrieve.
Accepted values:
- AutomationInstances
- Automations
- AdvancedSettings
- Apps
- CustomAttribute
- EndpointGroupMembers
- EndpointGroups
- Me
- Endpoint
- EndpointApps
- Endpoints
- Logs
- MissingUpdates
- Organizations
- Packages
- PackageVersions
- Policy
- Policies
- PolicyResults
- ReportData
- ReportExport
- Reports
- Scripts
- AgentDeployment
- Vulnerabilities
- RawURI
- Settings
When this parameter is set to `RawURI`, the `URI` parameter must also be specified. When this parameter is set to `Settings`, the `For` parameter must also be specified.
Specifies the identifier of the Action1 object to retrieve.
Use this parameter with queries that retrieve a specific resource instance rather than a list.
Specifies the maximum number of records requested per paged API call.

This parameter maps to the API page-size limit used by paged requests. If this parameter is not specified or is set to `0`, the function uses a page size of 200. The value must be greater than or equal to 0.
Specifies a literal API path for a direct GET request.
This parameter is required when `Query` is set to `RawURI`.
Specifies the Action1 resource type when retrieving template objects.
This parameter is used when `Query` is set to `Settings`.
Accepted values:
- Automation
- Endpoint
- EndpointGroup
- Organization
- GroupAddEndpoint
- GroupDeleteEndpoint
- GroupFilter
- Remediation
- DeferredRemediation
- DeploySoftware
Specifies the ID of an existing Action1 object to clone when generating a template.
This parameter is only used when `Query` is set to `Settings`. The function supports cloning for selected template types, including endpoint groups and automations.
Specifies the ID of an existing Action1 object to clone when generating a template.
This parameter is only used when `Query` is set to `Settings`. The function supports cloning for selected template types, including endpoint groups and automations.
Specifies the Action1 resource type when retrieving template objects.
This parameter is used when `Query` is set to `Settings`.
Accepted values:
- Automation
- Endpoint
- EndpointGroup
- Organization
- GroupAddEndpoint
- GroupDeleteEndpoint
- GroupFilter
- Remediation
- DeferredRemediation
- DeploySoftware
Specifies the identifier of the Action1 object to retrieve.
Use this parameter with queries that retrieve a specific resource instance rather than a list.
Specifies the maximum number of records requested per paged API call.

This parameter maps to the API page-size limit used by paged requests. If this parameter is not specified or is set to `0`, the function uses a page size of 200. The value must be greater than or equal to 0.
Specifies the type of Action1 object, data set, raw API request, or template to retrieve.
Accepted values:
- AutomationInstances
- Automations
- AdvancedSettings
- Apps
- CustomAttribute
- EndpointGroupMembers
- EndpointGroups
- Me
- Endpoint
- EndpointApps
- Endpoints
- Logs
- MissingUpdates
- Organizations
- Packages
- PackageVersions
- Policy
- Policies
- PolicyResults
- ReportData
- ReportExport
- Reports
- Scripts
- AgentDeployment
- Vulnerabilities
- RawURI
- Settings
When this parameter is set to `RawURI`, the `URI` parameter must also be specified. When this parameter is set to `Settings`, the `For` parameter must also be specified.
Specifies a literal API path for a direct GET request.
This parameter is required when `Query` is set to `RawURI`.
You cannot pipe objects to this command.

Returns objects retrieved from the Action1 API or template objects created by the function. The returned object type depends on the value of the `Query` parameter. Some returned objects may include helper script methods for additional operations.

maml:alert.maml:para: Valid API credentials must be configured before using this command. Use `Set-Action1Credentials` to configure API credentials.
The default Action1 organization should be configured with `Set-Action1DefaultOrg` before running organization-scoped queries.
Pagination is automatically processed for long list queries. Some queries return raw API responses, while others return processed objects with helper methods.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns all endpoints belonging to the current default Action1 organization.
Retrieves a specific Action1 endpoint by ID.
Performs a direct Action1 API GET request using the specified API path.
Returns a new Action1 endpoint group template object that can be customized and used with `New-Action1`.
Creates a new Action1 endpoint group template cloned from an existing endpoint group.
Retrieves Action1 policy results and then fetches the detailed records for each result.
Retrieves Action1 endpoints with an API page size of 50 objects.
Returns a deployment template object used to prepare a software deployment request.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new Action1 object.
`New-Action1` creates a new object in Action1 by sending a POST request to the Action1 API.

The object type is selected with the Item parameter. For supported item types, the function resolves the API endpoint from the module's internal URI lookup table and sends the supplied Data object as the request body.
When Item is set to `RawURI`, the function bypasses the internal URI lookup table and sends the POST request directly to the API path provided in URI .
The command requires valid Action1 authentication. For organization-scoped item types, the default organization must also be configured before running this command.
Specifies the type of Action1 object to create.
Accepted values:
- EndpointGroup
- Organization
- Automation
- Remediation
- DeferredRemediation
- DeploySoftware
- RawURI
When this parameter is set to `RawURI`, the URI parameter must also be supplied.
Specifies a literal Action1 API path used for a raw POST request.
This parameter is required when Item is set to `RawURI`. For all other Item values, the function determines the API path automatically from the module's internal URI lookup table.
Specifies the request body to send to the Action1 API.

The required object structure depends on the selected Item value and the corresponding Action1 API endpoint. Template objects for several supported item types can be retrieved with `Get-Action1 -Query Settings -For <Type>`.
Specifies the request body to send to the Action1 API.

The required object structure depends on the selected Item value and the corresponding Action1 API endpoint. Template objects for several supported item types can be retrieved with `Get-Action1 -Query Settings -For <Type>`.
Specifies the type of Action1 object to create.
Accepted values:
- EndpointGroup
- Organization
- Automation
- Remediation
- DeferredRemediation
- DeploySoftware
- RawURI
When this parameter is set to `RawURI`, the URI parameter must also be supplied.
Specifies a literal Action1 API path used for a raw POST request.
This parameter is required when Item is set to `RawURI`. For all other Item values, the function determines the API path automatically from the module's internal URI lookup table.
You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns the response object produced by the Action1 API request. If the API request fails, or if Item is `RawURI` and URI is not supplied, the function returns `$null`.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command sends data using an HTTP POST request through the module's internal API request helper.
Use `Set-Action1Credentials` before running this command.
For organization-scoped item types, set the default organization before running this command.
When using `RawURI`, provide a valid API path in URI .

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new Action1 organization using the data in `$OrgData`.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $GroupData = Get-Action1 -Query Settings -For EndpointGroup PS C:\> $GroupData.name = 'Production Servers' PS C:\> New-Action1 -Item EndpointGroup -Data $GroupData

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new Action1 endpoint group from an endpoint group template object.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $AutomationData = Get-Action1 -Query Settings -For Automation PS C:\> $AutomationData.name = 'Weekly maintenance' PS C:\> New-Action1 -Item Automation -Data $AutomationData

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new Action1 automation from an automation template object.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sends a POST request directly to the specified API path using the supplied payload.

maml:description.maml:para: Deprecated wrapper for `Set-Action1Region`. Sets the Action1 API region used by the PSAction1 module for the current PowerShell session.
`Set-Action1Locale` is deprecated and is retained only for backward compatibility with earlier versions of PSAction1.
The function forwards the supplied Region value to `Set-Action1Region`, which configures the Action1 API region endpoint used by subsequent module commands.
Use `Set-Action1Region` in new scripts and automation.
Specifies the Action1 API region to use.
The value is passed directly to `Set-Action1Region`.
Accepted values:
- NorthAmerica
- NorthAmerica-2
- NA-2
- Europe
- Australia
Specifies the Action1 API region to use.
The value is passed directly to `Set-Action1Region`.
Accepted values:
- NorthAmerica
- NorthAmerica-2
- NA-2
- Europe
- Australia
You cannot pipe objects to this command.
This command does not write objects to the pipeline. It calls `Set-Action1Region`, which updates the module's internal region setting for the current PowerShell session.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command is deprecated and may be removed in a future version of the PSAction1 module.
Use `Set-Action1Region` instead.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the Action1 API region to `NorthAmerica` for the current PowerShell session.
Sets the Action1 API region to `Europe` for the current PowerShell session.
Sets the Action1 API region using the `NA-2` region value. This command is deprecated; use `Set-Action1Region` instead.

maml:description.maml:para: Uploads a package file to an Action1 software repository package version.
`Start-Action1PackageUpload` uploads a local package file to an existing Action1 software repository package version.
The command opens a resumable upload stream for the specified package and version, then sends the file in chunks by using HTTP `PUT` requests with `Content-Range` headers.
The package version and target platform are selected by the Package_ID , Version_ID , and Platform parameters. The Platform value must be either `Windows_32` or `Windows_64`.

The BufferSize parameter controls the maximum chunk size, in bytes. The default value is `24MB`. If the file is smaller than the configured buffer size, the command automatically reduces the buffer size to match the file length.
The command requires Action1 authentication to be configured before it is run.
Specifies the identifier of the Action1 software repository package.
The package must already exist in the selected Action1 organization.
Specifies the identifier of the package version that receives the uploaded file.
The version must already exist for the package specified by Package_ID .
Specifies the path to the local package file to upload.
The file must exist and must be readable by the current PowerShell session.
Specifies the target platform for the uploaded package file.
Accepted values:
- Windows_32
- Windows_64
Specifies the upload chunk buffer size, in bytes.
Larger values reduce the number of upload requests but increase memory usage. Smaller values reduce memory usage but require more upload requests.
PowerShell size literals, such as `24MB` or `48MB`, can be used when calling the command.
Specifies the upload chunk buffer size, in bytes.
Larger values reduce the number of upload requests but increase memory usage. Smaller values reduce memory usage but require more upload requests.
PowerShell size literals, such as `24MB` or `48MB`, can be used when calling the command.
Specifies the path to the local package file to upload.
The file must exist and must be readable by the current PowerShell session.
Specifies the identifier of the Action1 software repository package.
The package must already exist in the selected Action1 organization.
Specifies the target platform for the uploaded package file.
Accepted values:
- Windows_32
- Windows_64
Specifies the identifier of the package version that receives the uploaded file.
The version must already exist for the package specified by Package_ID .
You cannot pipe objects to this command.
This command performs the upload operation and writes progress information through the module debug output.

maml:alert.maml:para: The command requires a valid Action1 authentication token in the current session. Use `Set-Action1Credentials` before running this command.
The selected package and package version must exist before the upload is started.
The upload uses a resumable chunked transfer workflow. During upload, progress is reported through `Write-Action1Debug` messages.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Uploads `C:\Packages\App.msi` to package `12345`, version `1.0.0`, for the Windows 64-bit platform.
Uploads `.\installer.exe` to package `12345`, version `2.0.0`, for the Windows 32-bit platform. The upload uses a 48 MB chunk buffer.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $packageId = "12345" PS C:\> $versionId = "67890" PS C:\> Start-Action1PackageUpload -Package_ID $packageId -Version_ID $versionId -Filename "C:\Installers\agent-x64.msi" -Platform Windows_64

dev:remarks.maml:para: Stores the package and version identifiers in variables, then uploads the package file for the Windows 64-bit platform.

maml:description.maml:para: Triggers an Action1 requery operation to refresh report, software inventory, or update data.
`Start-Action1Requery` sends a POST request to the Action1 API to refresh a supported dataset.

The command uses the module's internal URI map to build the correct requery endpoint for the selected Type value. Some requery operations run at the default organization level. Other operations can target a specific endpoint when Endpoint_Id is supplied.
If Endpoint_Id is specified for a requery type that does not support endpoint targeting, the command writes an error and continues by using the organization-wide requery endpoint.
Supported requery types are:
- ReportData - Refreshes report data. - InstalledSoftware - Refreshes installed software inventory data. - InstalledUpdates - Refreshes installed update data.
Authentication must be configured before using this command. For organization-scoped requery operations, the default Action1 organization must also be set.
Specifies the type of requery operation to perform.
Accepted values:
- ReportData
- InstalledSoftware
- InstalledUpdates
Specifies the endpoint identifier for an endpoint-specific requery operation.
When this parameter is omitted, the command runs the requery at the organization level.
If the selected requery type does not support endpoint targeting, this parameter is ignored after an error is written, and the command continues by using the organization-wide requery endpoint.
Specifies the endpoint identifier for an endpoint-specific requery operation.
When this parameter is omitted, the command runs the requery at the organization level.
If the selected requery type does not support endpoint targeting, this parameter is ignored after an error is written, and the command continues by using the organization-wide requery endpoint.
Specifies the type of requery operation to perform.
Accepted values:
- ReportData
- InstalledSoftware
- InstalledUpdates
You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns the API response from the requery request.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command requires a valid Action1 authentication token in the current session.
Use `Set-Action1Credentials` before invoking API actions.
For organization-scoped requests, set the default organization by using `Set-Action1DefaultOrg` before running this command.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Triggers a refresh of report data for the default Action1 organization.
Triggers a refresh of installed software inventory data for the specified Action1 endpoint.
Triggers a refresh of installed update data for the default Action1 organization.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates, modifies endpoint group membership, or deletes Action1 objects.
`Update-Action1` performs update operations against Action1 objects exposed through the Action1 API.
Depending on the values of the Action and Type parameters, the command can:
- Modify properties of endpoints, endpoint groups, and automations.
- Modify endpoint group membership.
- Update an endpoint custom attribute.
- Delete endpoints, endpoint groups, and automations.
- Send a raw PATCH request to a specified API URI.

The function builds the appropriate API endpoint path internally and sends the request by using the module's internal API helper functions. Authentication must be configured in advance by using `Set-Action1Credentials`. For operations that target organization resources, the default organization must be configured in advance by using `Set-Action1DefaultOrg`.
For destructive actions such as `Delete`, the command prompts for confirmation unless the Force switch is specified.
When Type is `RawURI`, the command sends a PATCH request directly to the URI specified by the URI parameter.
Specifies the type of operation to perform.
Accepted values are:
- Modify
- ModifyMembers
- Delete
Specifies the target object type for the operation.
Accepted values are:
- EndpointGroup
- Endpoint
- Automation
- CustomAttribute
- RawURI
Specifies the request body sent to the API.

For `Modify` operations, this parameter contains the properties to update. When modifying endpoints, only the `name` and `comment` properties are sent to the API. For `ModifyMembers` operations, this parameter contains endpoint group membership data. For `RawURI` requests, this parameter contains the raw PATCH request body.
Specifies the identifier of the Action1 object being modified or deleted.

This parameter is required by the function logic for most `Modify` operations and is also needed for `ModifyMembers` and `Delete` operations that target a specific object. For `CustomAttribute` updates, this parameter specifies the endpoint ID.
Specifies the name of the custom attribute to update.
This parameter is used when Action is `Modify` and Type is `CustomAttribute`.
Specifies the value to assign to the custom attribute.
This parameter is used when Action is `Modify` and Type is `CustomAttribute`.
Specifies the raw API URI to send the request to.
This parameter is required when Type is `RawURI`. When Type is `RawURI`, the function sends a PATCH request directly to this URI and bypasses the internal URI lookup table.
Suppresses the confirmation prompt when performing a `Delete` action.
Specifies the type of operation to perform.
Accepted values are:
- Modify
- ModifyMembers
- Delete
Specifies the name of the custom attribute to update.
This parameter is used when Action is `Modify` and Type is `CustomAttribute`.
Specifies the value to assign to the custom attribute.
This parameter is used when Action is `Modify` and Type is `CustomAttribute`.
Specifies the request body sent to the API.

For `Modify` operations, this parameter contains the properties to update. When modifying endpoints, only the `name` and `comment` properties are sent to the API. For `ModifyMembers` operations, this parameter contains endpoint group membership data. For `RawURI` requests, this parameter contains the raw PATCH request body.
Suppresses the confirmation prompt when performing a `Delete` action.
Specifies the identifier of the Action1 object being modified or deleted.

This parameter is required by the function logic for most `Modify` operations and is also needed for `ModifyMembers` and `Delete` operations that target a specific object. For `CustomAttribute` updates, this parameter specifies the endpoint ID.
Specifies the target object type for the operation.
Accepted values are:
- EndpointGroup
- Endpoint
- Automation
- CustomAttribute
- RawURI
Specifies the raw API URI to send the request to.
This parameter is required when Type is `RawURI`. When Type is `RawURI`, the function sends a PATCH request directly to this URI and bypasses the internal URI lookup table.
You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns the response object from the Action1 API request.

maml:alert.maml:para: The command requires a valid authentication token. Use `Set-Action1Credentials` before running API operations.
For operations that target organization resources, configure the default organization by using `Set-Action1DefaultOrg`.
Endpoint modifications automatically remove properties other than `name` and `comment` from the request body before sending the request to the API.
`Delete` operations prompt for confirmation unless the Force switch is specified.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Updates the name of the Action1 endpoint with the ID `endpoint-123`.

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 2: Update an endpoint comment ------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Updates the comment for the Action1 endpoint with the ID `endpoint-123`.
Updates properties of the Action1 endpoint group with the ID `group-42`.
Sends endpoint membership data for the Action1 endpoint group with the ID `group-42`.

command:example.maml:title: -------- Example 5: Update an endpoint custom attribute --------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the custom attribute named `Owner` to `IT` on the Action1 endpoint with the ID `endpoint-123`.

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 6: Delete an automation after confirmation ------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Prompts for confirmation and deletes the Action1 automation with the ID `auto-88` if the operation is confirmed.

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 7: Delete an endpoint without prompting -------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Deletes the Action1 endpoint with the ID `endpoint-123` without prompting for confirmation.

command:example.maml:title: ------------- Example 8: Send a raw PATCH request -------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sends a PATCH request directly to the Action1 API URI `/v1/custom/path`.

maml:description.maml:para: Exports Action1 vulnerability endpoint details to a CSV file.
Exports a CSV report that maps Action1 vulnerabilities to affected endpoints in the current Action1 organization.

The command can export endpoint details for all vulnerabilities returned by Get-Action1Vulnerabilities , optionally filtered by remediation status and severity score, or it can export endpoint details only for explicitly specified CVE IDs.
For each selected CVE ID, the command retrieves affected endpoints by using Get-Action1VulnerabilityEndpoints . When available, vulnerability metadata is retrieved by using Get-Action1Vulnerability .
The command writes the following CSV columns:
* `CVEId`
* `CVSSScore`
* `Severity`
* `RemediationStatus`
* `EndpointId`
* `EndpointName`
* `ApplicationName`
* `InstalledVersion`
* `AvailableUpdate`
The command creates the target directory when it does not already exist and overwrites the target CSV file if it already exists.
Use Force to write to the target file when file attributes, such as read-only or hidden, would otherwise prevent writing. Force does not override file locks or insufficient file system permissions.
The command uses the module default organization configured by Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
Specifies the path to the CSV file to create.
If the path contains a directory that does not exist, the command creates the directory. If the file already exists, the command overwrites it.
If the existing target file has read-only or hidden file attributes, use Force .
If this parameter is not specified, the command creates a timestamped CSV file in the current location using the `Action1__VulnerabilitiesEndpoints_yyyyMMdd_HHmmss.csv` naming format.
Specifies one or more CVE IDs whose affected endpoint records should be exported.
Each value must match the `CVE-YYYY-NNN` or `CVE-YYYY-NNNNNN` format.
When this parameter is specified, the command uses only the provided CVE IDs. RemediationStatus and Score are not used to select CVEs.
Specifies the remediation status used to select vulnerabilities when CVEIds is not specified.
The default value is `All`.
The acceptable values for this parameter are:
* `Overdue`
* `Due_soon`
* `Overdue_due_soon`
* `Due_later`
* `Control_applied`
* `All_except_control_applied`
* `All`
Specifies the vulnerability severity score used to select vulnerabilities when CVEIds is not specified.
The default value is `All`.
The acceptable values for this parameter are:
* `Critical`
* `High`
* `Medium`
* `Low`
* `All`
Forces the command to write to the target CSV file when file attributes, such as read-only or hidden, would otherwise prevent writing.

This parameter does not override file locks or insufficient file system permissions. Close the file if it is open in another application, such as Microsoft Excel, and verify that you have write permission to the target location.
Specifies one or more CVE IDs whose affected endpoint records should be exported.
Each value must match the `CVE-YYYY-NNN` or `CVE-YYYY-NNNNNN` format.
When this parameter is specified, the command uses only the provided CVE IDs. RemediationStatus and Score are not used to select CVEs.
Specifies the path to the CSV file to create.
If the path contains a directory that does not exist, the command creates the directory. If the file already exists, the command overwrites it.
If the existing target file has read-only or hidden file attributes, use Force .
If this parameter is not specified, the command creates a timestamped CSV file in the current location using the `Action1__VulnerabilitiesEndpoints_yyyyMMdd_HHmmss.csv` naming format.
Specifies the remediation status used to select vulnerabilities when CVEIds is not specified.
The default value is `All`.
The acceptable values for this parameter are:
* `Overdue`
* `Due_soon`
* `Overdue_due_soon`
* `Due_later`
* `Control_applied`
* `All_except_control_applied`
* `All`
Specifies the vulnerability severity score used to select vulnerabilities when CVEIds is not specified.
The default value is `All`.
The acceptable values for this parameter are:
* `Critical`
* `High`
* `Medium`
* `Low`
* `All`
Forces the command to write to the target CSV file when file attributes, such as read-only or hidden, would otherwise prevent writing.

This parameter does not override file locks or insufficient file system permissions. Close the file if it is open in another application, such as Microsoft Excel, and verify that you have write permission to the target location.
You cannot pipe input to this command.
This command does not return pipeline output. It creates or overwrites a CSV file at the specified path.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires the default Action1 organization to be configured with Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
Requires permission to view vulnerabilities in Action1.
The command displays progress while processing CVE IDs.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Export all vulnerability endpoint records to the default CSV file

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports vulnerability endpoint records to a timestamped CSV file in the current location.

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Export critical vulnerability endpoint records to a specific file

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports endpoint records for vulnerabilities with a critical score to the specified CSV file.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 3: Export overdue vulnerability endpoint records ---

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports endpoint records for vulnerabilities with an overdue remediation status to the specified CSV file.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 4: Export endpoint records for specific CVE IDs ---

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports endpoint records only for the specified CVE IDs.
When CVEIds is specified, RemediationStatus and Score are not used to select CVEs.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 5: Export and import the CSV data for review -----
command:example.dev:code: $Path = 'C:\Reports\VulnerabilityEndpoints.csv' Export-Action1VulnerabilitiesEndpointsCsv -Path $Path -RemediationStatus All_except_control_applied -Score High Import-Csv -Path $Path | Format-Table CVEId, Severity, EndpointName, ApplicationName, InstalledVersion, AvailableUpdate

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports high severity vulnerability endpoint records, excluding vulnerabilities with applied compensating controls, and displays selected CSV columns.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 6: Export to a read-only or hidden CSV file -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports vulnerability endpoint records and attempts to write to the target file even when file attributes, such as read-only or hidden, would otherwise prevent writing.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets vulnerabilities for the current Action1 organization.
Gets vulnerable software records from the current Action1 organization by using the Action1 vulnerabilities API.
By default, the command returns vulnerabilities with the remediation status `Control_applied` and severity score `Critical`.
Use RemediationStatus to filter vulnerabilities by remediation status.
Use Score to filter vulnerabilities by severity score.
To disable one of these filters, specify `All` for the corresponding parameter.
The command uses the module default organization configured by Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
Filters vulnerabilities by remediation status.
Specify `All` to disable remediation status filtering.
Accepted values:
* `Overdue`
* `Due_soon`
* `Overdue_due_soon`
* `Due_later`
* `Control_applied`
* `All_except_control_applied`
* `All`
Filters vulnerabilities by severity score.
Specify `All` to disable score filtering.
Accepted values:
* `Critical`
* `High`
* `Medium`
* `Low`
* `All`
Filters vulnerabilities by remediation status.
Specify `All` to disable remediation status filtering.
Accepted values:
* `Overdue`
* `Due_soon`
* `Overdue_due_soon`
* `Due_later`
* `Control_applied`
* `All_except_control_applied`
* `All`
Filters vulnerabilities by severity score.
Specify `All` to disable score filtering.
Accepted values:
* `Critical`
* `High`
* `Medium`
* `Low`
* `All`
You cannot pipe input to this command.
Returns vulnerability objects from Action1.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires the default Action1 organization to be configured with Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
The command retrieves paged results from the Action1 API and returns vulnerability objects to the pipeline.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Get critical vulnerabilities with compensating controls applied

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets vulnerabilities in the current organization where the remediation status is `Control_applied` and the score is `Critical`.
This is the default behavior.
Gets all vulnerabilities visible to the authenticated Action1 account in the current organization without filtering by remediation status or score.

command:example.maml:title: Example 3: Get all vulnerabilities with compensating controls applied

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets vulnerabilities where the remediation status is `Control_applied`, regardless of severity score.
Gets overdue vulnerabilities with the default score filter `Critical`.

command:example.maml:title: Example 5: Get all overdue vulnerabilities regardless of score

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets overdue vulnerabilities regardless of severity score.

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 6: Get high severity vulnerabilities due soon ----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets vulnerabilities where the remediation status is `Due_soon` and the score is `High`.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 7: Review overdue vulnerabilities ----------
command:example.dev:code: Get-Action1Vulnerabilities -RemediationStatus Overdue -Score All | Select-Object cve_id, name, remediation_status, score

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets overdue vulnerabilities and selects key fields.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets one Action1 vulnerability by CVE ID.
Gets detailed information about a specific vulnerability in the current Action1 organization.
The command uses the module default organization configured by Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
Specifies the CVE ID of the vulnerability to retrieve.
Specifies the CVE ID of the vulnerability to retrieve.
You cannot pipe input to this command.
Returns the vulnerability object returned by Action1.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires the default Action1 organization to be configured with Set-Action1DefaultOrg .

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 1: Get a vulnerability by CVE ID -----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets details for `CVE-2024-12345` in the current Action1 organization.
Gets the vulnerability and displays all returned fields.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets endpoints affected by an Action1 vulnerability.
Gets endpoints in the current Action1 organization that are affected by a specific vulnerability.
The command uses the module default organization configured by Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
The CVE ID must use the standard CVE format, such as `CVE-2024-12345`.
Specifies the CVE ID whose affected endpoints should be retrieved.
The value must match the `CVE-YYYY-NNN` or `CVE-YYYY-NNNNNN` format.
Specifies the CVE ID whose affected endpoints should be retrieved.
The value must match the `CVE-YYYY-NNN` or `CVE-YYYY-NNNNNN` format.
You cannot pipe input to this command.
Returns vulnerable endpoint objects from Action1.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires the default Action1 organization to be configured with Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
Requires permission to view vulnerabilities in Action1.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 1: Get endpoints affected by a vulnerability -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets endpoints affected by `CVE-2024-12345`.
Gets the affected endpoints and displays all returned fields.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets remediation records for an Action1 vulnerability.
Gets previously applied remediation records, including compensating control records, for a specific vulnerability in the current Action1 organization.
The command uses the module default organization configured by Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
The CVE ID must use the standard CVE format, such as `CVE-2024-12345`.
Specifies the CVE ID whose remediation records should be retrieved.
Specifies the CVE ID whose remediation records should be retrieved.
You cannot pipe input to this command.
Returns remediation objects from Action1.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires the default Action1 organization to be configured with Set-Action1DefaultOrg .

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 1: Get remediation records for a vulnerability ----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets remediation records for `CVE-2024-12345`.

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 2: Display remediation records before deleting ----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the remediation records and displays all returned fields.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a compensating control remediation record for an Action1 vulnerability.
Creates a new remediation record for a specific vulnerability in the current Action1 organization.
The command sends a POST request to the Action1 vulnerabilities remediation API endpoint for the specified CVE ID.
The request body includes the following values: `comment` - The value of the Comment * parameter. `product_name` - The value of the ProductName * parameter.
The command uses the module default organization configured by Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
Specifies the CVE ID of the vulnerability for which the remediation record should be created.
The value must match the CVE format `CVE-YYYY-NNN`, where:
* `YYYY` is a 4-digit year.
* `NNN` is a 3- to 6-digit CVE number.
Examples:
Specifies the remediation comment text to send to Action1.
The value is sent in the request body as `comment`.
Specifies the product name associated with the compensating control remediation.
The value is sent in the request body as `product_name`.
Specifies the CVE ID of the vulnerability for which the remediation record should be created.
The value must match the CVE format `CVE-YYYY-NNN`, where:
* `YYYY` is a 4-digit year.
* `NNN` is a 3- to 6-digit CVE number.
Examples:
Specifies the remediation comment text to send to Action1.
The value is sent in the request body as `comment`.
Specifies the product name associated with the compensating control remediation.
The value is sent in the request body as `product_name`.
You cannot pipe input to this command.
Returns the remediation object returned by Action1.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires the default Action1 organization to be configured with Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
Requires permission to manage vulnerabilities in Action1.
The command uses Write-Action1Debug for debug logging.

command:example.maml:title: - Example 1: Create a compensating control remediation record -
command:example.dev:code: New-Action1CompensatingControlRemediation ` -CVEId 'CVE-2024-12345' ` -Comment 'The vulnerable product is isolated and blocked from internet access.' ` -ProductName 'Example Product'

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a compensating control remediation record for `CVE-2024-12345`.

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Create a remediation record and review the returned object
command:example.dev:code: New-Action1CompensatingControlRemediation ` -CVEId 'CVE-2024-12345' ` -Comment 'The vulnerable component is not reachable from untrusted networks.' ` -ProductName 'Example Product' | Format-List

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates the remediation record and displays all fields returned by Action1.

maml:description.maml:para: Deletes one remediation record for an Action1 vulnerability.
Deletes a specific remediation record for a specific vulnerability in the current Action1 organization.
The Action1 API requires both the CVE ID and the remediation ID to delete a remediation record.
The CVE ID must use the standard CVE format, such as `CVE-2024-12345`.
This command supports PowerShell confirmation. Use -WhatIf to preview the delete operation. Use -Force to bypass the confirmation prompt.
Specifies the CVE ID that owns the remediation record.
Specifies the remediation record ID to delete.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the command.
Bypasses the confirmation prompt. -WhatIf is still honored when it is specified.
Shows what would happen if the command runs. The command is not run.
Specifies the CVE ID that owns the remediation record.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the command.
Bypasses the confirmation prompt. -WhatIf is still honored when it is specified.
Specifies the remediation record ID to delete.
Shows what would happen if the command runs. The command is not run.
You cannot pipe input to this command.
Returns a status object with CVEId, RemediationId, Status, and Response.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires permission to manage vulnerabilities in Action1.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Prompts for confirmation, then deletes the specified remediation record.
Shows what would be deleted without sending the DELETE request.
Deletes the remediation record without prompting for confirmation.

maml:description.maml:para: Deletes compensating control remediation records for vulnerabilities with remediation status `Control_applied`.
Finds vulnerabilities in the current Action1 organization where the remediation status is `Control_applied`.
The command processes vulnerabilities across all severity scores. Internally, it queries vulnerabilities with remediation status `Control_applied` and score `All` by default.
For each matching vulnerability, the command gets remediation records, displays the remediation records that are candidates for deletion, and deletes each remediation record after confirmation.
This command supports PowerShell confirmation. Use -WhatIf to preview delete operations. Use -Force to bypass confirmation prompts.
The command uses the module default organization configured by Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
Filters vulnerabilities by severity score.
Specify `All` to disable score filtering and remove all severity score remediation records.
Accepted values:
* `Critical`
* `High`
* `Medium`
* `Low`
* `All`
Prompts you for confirmation before running the command.
Bypasses confirmation prompts for individual remediation deletions. -WhatIf is still honored when it is specified.
Shows what would happen if the command runs. The command is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the command.
Bypasses confirmation prompts for individual remediation deletions. -WhatIf is still honored when it is specified.
Filters vulnerabilities by severity score.
Specify `All` to disable score filtering and remove all severity score remediation records.
Accepted values:
* `Critical`
* `High`
* `Medium`
* `Low`
* `All`
Shows what would happen if the command runs. The command is not run.
You cannot pipe input to this command.
Returns a summary object with counts for processed vulnerabilities and remediation deletion results.
The summary object includes the following properties:
* `VulnerabilitiesProcessed`
* `RemediationsFound`
* `RemediationsRemoved`
* `RemediationsSkipped`
* `RemediationsFailed`
* `RemediationStatus`

maml:alert.maml:para: Deleting remediation records is irreversible. Test with -WhatIf before using -Force .
This command is intentionally scoped to vulnerabilities with remediation status `Control_applied`.
This command processes all severity scores.
Requires permission to view and manage vulnerabilities in Action1.
Requires the default Action1 organization to be configured with Set-Action1DefaultOrg .

command:example.maml:title: -- Example 1: Remove compensating control remediation records --

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets vulnerabilities with remediation status `Control_applied` across all severity scores, displays remediation records to delete, prompts for confirmation, and deletes selected records.

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Remove compensating control remediation records with all severity scores explicitly

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets vulnerabilities with remediation status `Control_applied` across all severity scores, displays remediation records to delete, prompts for confirmation, and deletes selected records.

command:example.maml:title: Example 3: Remove critical compensating control remediation records

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets vulnerabilities with remediation status `Control_applied` and `Critical` severity scores, displays remediation records to delete, prompts for confirmation, and deletes selected records.
Shows delete operations that would be performed without deleting remediation records.
Deletes remediation records for vulnerabilities with remediation status `Control_applied` across all severity scores without prompting for confirmation.
Deletes matching remediation records without prompting and stores the returned summary object in `$Summary`.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates the comment of one compensating control remediation record for an Action1 vulnerability.
Updates an existing compensating control remediation record for a specific vulnerability in the current Action1 organization.
The Action1 API allows updating only the remediation record comment. The command uses the module default organization configured by Set-Action1DefaultOrg .
Specifies the CVE ID that owns the remediation record.
The value must match the format `CVE-YYYY-NNN` through `CVE-YYYY-NNNNNN`, for example `CVE-2024-12345`.
Specifies the remediation record ID to update.
Specifies the new remediation comment.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the command.
Shows what would happen if the command runs. The command is not run.
Specifies the CVE ID that owns the remediation record.
The value must match the format `CVE-YYYY-NNN` through `CVE-YYYY-NNNNNN`, for example `CVE-2024-12345`.
Specifies the new remediation comment.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the command.
Specifies the remediation record ID to update.
Shows what would happen if the command runs. The command is not run.
You cannot pipe input to this command.
Returns the updated remediation object returned by Action1.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires permission to manage vulnerabilities in Action1.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 1: Update a remediation comment -----------
command:example.dev:code: Update-Action1CompensatingControlRemediation ` -CVEId 'CVE-2024-12345' ` -RemediationId '03445606-d55d-41c5-b877-b9a21618ae79' ` -Comment 'The application has been blocked from accessing the internet.'

dev:remarks.maml:para: Updates the comment for the specified compensating control remediation record.
Shows what would be updated without sending the PATCH request.
